Set up a Colab environment with GPU runtime enabled, preferably a T4 GPU
Install or upgrade to Keras version 3.2.1 and keras-nlp via the commands: `pip install -q --upgrade keras==3.2.1 keras-nlp`
Import TensorFlow, keras, keras_nlp, and other necessary modules (`import tensorflow as tf`, `import keras`, `import keras_nlp`, `import random`)
Define constants: BATCH_SIZE=64, SEQ_LEN=128, VOCAB_SIZE=5000, EMBED_DIM=256, FEED_FORWARD_DIM=128, NUM_HEADS=3, NUM_LAYERS=2, EPOCHS=1 (for quick reproduction)
Download and prepare a text dataset similar to the KerasNLP English-to-Spanish translation example, or alternatively use dummy text data that exceeds a minimum string length of 512 characters
Train a WordPieceTokenizer using `keras_nlp.tokenizers.compute_word_piece_vocabulary` on the dataset with reserved tokens: ['[PAD]', '[UNK]', '[BOS]'] and vocabulary size of VOCAB_SIZE
Initialize the WordPieceTokenizer with the computed vocabulary, sequence_length=SEQ_LEN, lowercase=True
Create a StartEndPacker layer with sequence_length=SEQ_LEN and start_value set to the tokenizer token ID for '[BOS]'
Define a preprocess function that tokenizes input sentences using the tokenizer, packs with the StartEndPacker, and returns (features, labels)
Prepare training and validation datasets using tf.data pipelines that batch and shuffle the data, and map the preprocess function

Construct a GPT-style model using keras Functional API: input layer with shape (None,), TokenAndPositionEmbedding layer (vocabulary_size=VOCAB_SIZE, sequence_length=SEQ_LEN, embedding_dim=EMBED_DIM), followed by NUM_LAYERS TransformerDecoder layers with num_heads=NUM_HEADS and intermediate_dim=FEED_FORWARD_DIM, then a Dense layer outputting VOCAB_SIZE logits
Compile the model using SparseCategoricalCrossentropy(from_logits=True) as loss, and keras_nlp.metrics.Perplexity as a metric with from_logits=True and mask_token_id=0
Train the model on the training dataset for EPOCHS epochs with validation on the validation dataset; verify training completes successfully without errors

Define a 'next' callback function that takes arguments (prompt, cache, index), calls the model on the prompt, and returns logits for the token at position index-1, along with hidden_states=None and the cache
Create a prompt sequence by tokenizing an empty string with the tokenizer and packing it with StartEndPacker to get prompt_tokens containing the '[BOS]' token

Attempt to perform inference by calling `keras_nlp.samplers.GreedySampler()` instance with the signature: sampler(next=next, prompt=prompt_tokens, index=1, end_token_id=some_token_id)` where `end_token_id` is set to the tokenizer token ID for '[PAD]' or '[EOS]' if defined
Observe the TypeError: `Sampler.__call__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'end_token_id'` thrown at this call

Assert that the error is reproducible on this minimal example and matches the exact error message and stack trace indicating that the GreedySampler (or more generally Sampler) __call__ method does not accept `end_token_id` as a parameter
